prerequisites: A Databricks workspace with a running SQL warehouse (or all-purpose compute) to connect to.
The workspace Server Hostname and HTTP Path from the SQL warehouse's Connection Details (used for the hostname and http_path fields).
The target Unity Catalog catalog (database field) and an existing schema, or permission to create the schema specified in the schema field.

Permission from a workspace admin: token authentication must be enabled and your user must be allowed to create tokens (for PAT), or a service principal with an OAuth secret must be available (for OAuth M2M).
Acceptance of the Databricks terms required by the destination (accept_terms field).

authMethods.name: Personal access token
authMethods.summary: Maps to the authentication.personal_access_token secret field. In the Databricks workspace, open Settings > Developer, click Manage next to Access tokens, then Generate new token, set a name and lifetime, and copy the displayed token (it is shown only once). Token authentication must be enabled by a workspace admin, and unused tokens are auto-revoked after 90 days.

authMethods.name: OAuth machine-to-machine (service principal)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the authentication.secret secret field. Create an OAuth secret for a Databricks service principal under Settings > Identity and access > Service principals > Manage, select the service principal, open the Secrets tab, and click Generate secret. Copy both the client ID and the secret value (the secret is shown only once). Each service principal supports up to five secrets with a maximum lifetime of 730 days.

setupSteps.title: Locate workspace connection details
setupSteps.body: In the Databricks workspace sidebar, go to SQL > SQL Warehouses and select your warehouse, then open the Connection Details tab. Copy the Server Hostname into the hostname field, the HTTP Path into the http_path field, and set port if it differs from the default. For all-purpose compute, find the same values under the cluster's Configuration > Advanced options > JDBC/ODBC tab.

setupSteps.title: Generate authentication credentials
setupSteps.body: Choose one method. For a personal access token, go to Settings > Developer > Access tokens > Manage > Generate new token and supply the value as authentication.personal_access_token. For OAuth machine-to-machine, generate a service principal OAuth secret under Settings > Identity and access > Service principals and supply the client ID and secret as authentication.secret.

setupSteps.title: Configure the catalog and schema
setupSteps.body: Set database to the Unity Catalog catalog to write to and schema to the target schema. Optionally set raw_schema_override to control where raw tables land, and purge_staging_data to control cleanup of staged files. Set accept_terms to confirm acceptance of the required Databricks terms.

setupSteps.title: Test the connection
setupSteps.body: Save the destination and run the connection test. The connector connects to the SQL warehouse using the hostname, http_path, and selected credential, then verifies access to the configured database (catalog) and schema.

sources.title: Databricks: Get connection details for a Databricks compute resource
Databricks: Authorize service principal access with OAuth (M2M)
